data.description:
## Why
Coverage across the three facades (MCP, CLI, library) is uneven after the migration. Every behavior, integration, and contract (internal AND external — incl. the Jira reconciler contract and the MCP output schemas) should be covered through all three interfaces where applicable.

## What
- Map behaviors/integrations/contracts × {MCP, CLI, lib}; find the gaps.
- Add tests to close gaps: parity across interfaces, external contracts (Jira reconciler field round-trips, MCP outputSchemas), internal contracts (optimistic-concurrency exit 10, lock discipline, replay determinism).
- Includes the roam-crone-disco follow-up (sign/verify-signature output schemas) if not already done.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Behavior×interface coverage matrix documented; gaps enumerated then closed
- [ ] External contracts covered: Jira reconciler bidirectional field round-trips; MCP outputSchema validation for every advertised command
- [ ] Internal contracts covered through CLI + lib + MCP: exit-10 concurrency, lock discipline, deterministic replay
- [ ] Coverage measured (coverage.py) with a recorded target; suite green locally + CI
data.title: Full behavior/integration/contract coverage across all 3 interfaces (MCP, CLI, lib)
